description: Computer networking - protocols, routing, transport, security.

facts.context: TCP congestion control AIMD stands for:
distractors: automatic interference mitigation and detection
always increase, maybe decrease on congestion
aggressive increase, minimal decrease strategy

facts.context: BGP path selection prefers routes with:
facts.truth: shortest AS path among valid routes
distractors: longest path for redundancy and reliability
random selection for load balancing purposes
most recently announced route always preferred
always 3-RTT minimum for security requirements
2-RTT exactly like TLS 1.2 handshake requires
variable RTTs depending on cipher suite selected

facts.context: QUIC's main advantage over TCP for HTTP is:
distractors: faster encryption than TLS can provide
smaller packet headers reducing overhead always
better compression ratios for all content types

facts.truth: authentication of DNS responses not encryption
distractors: encryption of all DNS query contents automatically
faster DNS resolution times through caching
automatic load balancing across DNS servers
nothing since NAT is fully transparent to applications
only protocols that use encryption for security
routing between networks using different protocols
only one packet sent before waiting for ACK
unlimited packets without any flow control
retransmission without acknowledgment needed

facts.truth: IP addresses to MAC addresses on local network
distractors: domain names to IP addresses like DNS does
MAC addresses to IP addresses in reverse
port numbers to process identifiers locally

facts.context: CDN edge caching improves performance by:
facts.truth: serving content from geographically closer servers
distractors: compressing all content more efficiently always
using faster protocols than the origin server
predicting user requests before they occur

facts.context: IP fragmentation occurs when packet size exceeds:
facts.truth: path MTU requiring split into smaller packets
distractors: destination buffer size requiring reassembly
source send buffer causing packet queuing
router memory limits dropping excess data

facts.context: OSPF is a link-state routing protocol that:
facts.truth: floods topology to all routers for SPF calculation
distractors: exchanges only distance vectors with neighbors
uses path-vector like BGP for route selection
requires manual configuration of all routes

facts.context: SYN flood attacks exhaust server resources by:
distractors: completing connections then sending huge payloads
encrypting traffic that server cannot decrypt
sending valid requests at extremely high rates
